data.title: Northern Star Plans Up to $346 Million Share Buyback
data.description:
(21:17 GMT) *Northern Star Resources: to Buy Back Up to A$500 Million in Shares
(21:18 GMT) *Northern Star: Buyback to Start About April 23; Be Completed Within 12 Months
(21:18 GMT) *Northern Star: Buyback Aligns With Capital Allocation Priorities
(21:19 GMT) *Northern Star: Buyback Won't Affect Dividend Policy to Pay Out 20-30% of Cash Earnings
(21:19 GMT) *Northern Star CEO: Buyback Reflects Confidence in Strength of Business
(21:21 GMT) *Northern Star CEO: Doesn't Believe Share Price Reflects Quality, Future Potential of Assets
(21:21 GMT) *Northern Star 3Q Gold Sales 381,000 Oz
(21:22 GMT) *Northern Star: Forecast to Deliver Revised FY26 Production Guidance
(21:22 GMT) *Northern Star: FY Result Remains Particularly Dependent on KCGM Mill Throughput
(21:22 GMT) *Northern Star: Not Currently Experiencing Any Diesel Fuel Supply Issues
(21:23 GMT) *Northern Star: Fuel Supply Remains a Focus For Business; Risk For Industry
(21:40 GMT) Northern Star Plans Up to $346 Million Share Buyback

By Rhiannon Hoyle

Northern Star Resources said it intends to buy up to 500 million Australian dollars, equivalent to US$346 million, of shares, reflecting its confidence in the strength of its business and a view that its stock is undervalued.

The Australian gold-mining company said the buyback will begin on or about April 23 and be completed within a year. It will not impact Northern Star's dividend policy to pay out between 20% and 30% of cash earnings, the company said.

The on-market buyback represents up to 1.6% of issued share capital, said Chief Executive Stuart Tonkin. The buyback is an efficient way to return capital to shareholders while being immediately earnings and value accretive, he said.

"Today's announcement reflects our confidence in the strength of our business, the structural uplift in cash generation expected from the commissioning of the KCGM Mill Expansion and the compelling value we see in our share price," Tonkin said.

In a separate announcement, Northern Star said the new KCGM mill remains on track for commissioning in early fiscal 2027.

It reported preliminary gold sales of 381,000 troy ounces for its third quarter of fiscal 2026 and said it expects to meet revised full-year production guidance of about 1.5 million ounces.

The company last month cut its annual gold production forecast for the second time in less than three months as it continued to grapple with operational challenges.

"As previously disclosed, this result remains particularly dependent on mill throughput at KCGM--with downside and upside potential," Northern Star said.

The miner added that it is not currently experiencing any supply issues with diesel fuel, but said that fuel supply remains a focus for the business and a key risk for the Australian mining industry more broadly.

Write to Rhiannon Hoyle at rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com

data.title: How TV's 'Love Story' helped Calvin Klein parent ease investor anxieties about consumer demand
data.description:
By Bill Peters

'When something like 'Love Story' hits, it's just a really nice sync-up with where we are with the brand,' PVH CEO says

"Love Story" stars, from left, Sarah Pidgeon, Grace Gummer, Paul Anthony Kelly and Naomi Watts at Carnegie Hall on Feb. 3.

Shares of PVH Corp., the parent of Calvin Klein and Tommy Hilfiger, finished nearly 10% higher on Wednesday, as the TV miniseries "Love Story: John F. Kennedy Jr. & Carolyn Bessette" and 1990s nostalgia help buoy consumer demand and the company's outlook through this year.

The company offered that assessment as Wall Street looks for signs that consumers can still endure higher costs of living over this decade, made more complicated over the past month by the Iran war, which has driven up oil and gas prices.

During PVH's (PVH) earnings call on Wednesday, CEO Stefan Larsson said it was impossible to talk about Calvin Klein without talking about "Love Story," a miniseries set in the 1990s that follows the romantic relationship between John F. Kennedy Jr. and Carolyn Bessette, who ascended the ranks at Calvin Klein.

"When the show launched, we could see the search increase for Calvin Klein, e-commerce traffic, D2C, is positive," Larsson said, referring to "direct-to-consumer." "The consumer is looking for iconic Calvin, starting with iconic underwear and iconic denim. The most-sold denim style right now is the 1990s fit."

"Love Story" premiered in February. According to a Variety report last month, it became FX's most-viewed limited series ever on Hulu and Disney+.

Larsson, during Wednesday's call, added that Calvin Klein had tried to take what made the brand popular in the 1990s and make it current.

"So when something like 'Love Story' hits, it's just a really nice sync-up with where we are with the brand," he said.

More broadly, Larsson said that trends so far for the spring season were encouraging, and that the company had started off this year with solid momentum. He said retailers that stock their shelves with PVH's clothes remain cautious, but added that fall-season ordering trends for Europe were "positive."

Europe, the Middle East and Africa drew more sales for PVH than in other regions during the company's last fiscal year, which ended on Feb. 1.

Last month, discount clothing retailer Ross Stores (ROST) also called out a "very strong start" for the spring shopping season. Nike (NKE), despite its struggles, said it had not yet seen any impact on consumer demand in North America due to the Iran war. However, heftier tax refunds expected this year, which analysts believed could help consumer spending at retailers, will likely get eaten up by higher gas prices.

PVH on Wednesday said it expects sales for the year ahead to "increase slightly" from last year. The company expects to earn $11.80 to $12.10 a share during that period, with the midpoint a bit above FactSet estimates for $11.93.

For its fourth quarter, PVH reported sales of around $2.51 billion, up 6%. The company reported adjusted earnings per share of $3.82. Both results topped Wall Street's estimates.

PVH's stock is still down around 1.6% over the past 12 months. But UBS analysts said in a note that they expect more strong results from the company.

"We think PVH has the brand strength and balance sheet to drive earnings growth over the long term," they said.

-Bill Peters

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Metalsource Mining Engages Investing News Network
data.description:
Vancouver, British Columbia--(Newsfile Corp. - April 1, 2026) - METALSOURCE MINING INC. (OTCQB: MSMMF) (FSE: E9Z) (the "Company" or "Metalsource") has engaged the services of Dig Media Inc. 'dba' Investing News Network ("INN") pursuant to an agreement dated and starting on April 1, 2026 (the "Agreement") and, for the 12-month term of the agreement, INN will provide advertising to increase awareness of the Company.

Under the terms of the Agreement, INN will receive a one-time payment of CAD $51,000, payable in advance. The Agreement is for an initial term of one year. There is no performance factors contained in the Agreement and INN will not receive shares or options as compensation. INN's address is 1200 - 736 Granville Street, Vancouver, BC, V6Z 1G3 (phone: 604-688-8231, email: info@investingnewsnetwork.com). INN and the Company are unrelated and unaffiliated entities.

INN is an arm's length private company headquartered in Vancouver, Canada, dedicated to providing independent news and education to investors since 2007 at www.investingnews.com.

About Metalsource Mining Inc.

Metalsource Mining Inc. is a Canadian mineral exploration company focused on advancing high-potential mineral assets through modern, systematic exploration and value-driven discovery.

For more information, please refer to SEDAR+ (www.sedarplus.ca), under the Company's profile.

Joseph Cullen, Chief Executive Officer and Director

data.title: Activist investor Jana Partners discloses 5% stake in Alkami Technology
data.description:
Activist investor Jana Partners has taken a 5.1% stake in Alkami Technology , as it believes that the banking technology company's shares are undervalued.

Jana owned 5.4 million shares of Alkami, a Plano, Texas-headquartered fintech provider for banks and credit unions, as of March 25, according to a filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission on Wednesday.

In November, Reuters had reported that the activist investor had taken a stake in Alkami in the third quarter.

By building the position, the hedge fund is signaling its interest in the company and that it has committed significant resources.

Jana has urged Alkami Technology to explore a sale to a rival or private equity firm, Bloomberg News reported in December.

data.title: Charlotte's Web Gets $10M Equity From British American Tobacco, Lowers Debenture Conversion to C$0.94
data.description:
Charlotte's Web announced a financing package with British American Tobacco that includes a US$10 million equity subscription and revised terms to its 2022 convertible debenture. The new equity will price at the greater of C$0.94 or the TSX-permitted discounted 5-day VWAP, with a maximum of 14,760,638 shares, and is expected to close on or around May 28, 2026. Concurrently, the debenture conversion price will be reset to C$0.94 and the conversion cap lifted to 40.8%, after which BAT will convert the full principal and accrued interest into 95,281,277 shares. The parties will also enter into an amended investor rights agreement granting board nominations tied to ownership, pre-emptive and top-up rights, a two-year standstill, an 18-month lock-up, and select consent rights.

Agreement 1: Charlotte's Web Sets $10M Equity Subscription With British American Tobacco at Floor C$0.94

  • Agreement type: Equity subscription agreement for up to 14.76M common shares
  • Counterparty: British American Tobacco
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 30 2026 / Mar 30 2026
  • Duration / Termination: N/A
  • Reason: Raise growth capital and strengthen balance sheet

Agreement 2: Charlotte's Web and British American Tobacco Amend Debenture; Conversion Price Reset to C$0.94

  • Agreement type: Amendment and conversion notice to 2022 convertible debenture (C$75.3M principal)
  • Counterparty: British American Tobacco
  • Signed / Effective: May 28 2026 / May 28 2026
  • Duration / Termination: N/A
  • Reason: Enable immediate conversion and align economics

Agreement 3: British American Tobacco to Convert Debenture Into 95.3M Charlotte's Web Shares

  • Agreement type: Debenture conversion notice (principal and accrued interest into shares)
  • Counterparty: British American Tobacco
  • Signed / Effective: May 28 2026 / May 28 2026
  • Duration / Termination: N/A
  • Reason: Reduce debt and simplify capital structure

Agreement 4: Charlotte's Web and British American Tobacco Agree to Amended Investor Rights With Board Seats

  • Agreement type: Amended and Restated Investor Rights Agreement
  • Counterparty: British American Tobacco
  • Signed / Effective: May 28 2026 / May 28 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Standstill 2 years; lock-up 18 months; rights while ≥10% ownership
  • Reason: Define governance and protect minority interests

Original SEC Filing: Charlotte's Web Holdings, Inc. [ CWBHF ] - 8-K - Apr. 01, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Eli Lilly May Have Found Its Edge In Weight Loss Race
data.description:
Eli Lilly is starting to show how it plans to take its weight-loss franchise global, and it may come down to something pretty simple: ease of manufacturing.

CEO Dave Ricks said the company's new GLP-1 pill, Foundayo, could be a game changer because it's much easier to produce at scale compared to current injectable drugs. That matters more than it sounds. Supply has been one of the biggest bottlenecks in this space.

Unlike drugs like Zepbound or Novo Nordisk's Wegovy, which are peptide-based and more complex to manufacture, Foundayo is a small molecule pill. That makes it simpler to produce in large volumes and easier to distribute globally.

Ricks made it clear this is the strategy. Lilly wants to launch the drug worldwide as quickly as approvals come through, with expectations for more than 40 countries potentially greenlighting it this year.

There's also a competitive angle here. If Lilly can solve the supply issue better than others, it could widen its lead in the fast-growing obesity market.

data.description:

data.title: ServiceNow's Deal May Be Bigger Than It Looks
data.description:
ServiceNow is making a move that could look small on the surface, but analysts think it carries bigger strategic weight.

Morgan Stanley says the company's planned acquisition of Armis isn't just about adding revenue. It's about filling a gap. ServiceNow has built a strong position managing digital workflows, but its visibility into physical assets has been relatively limited. That's where Armis comes in.

Armis specializes in tracking and securing physical and connected devices, especially in operational technology environments. By bringing that into its platform, ServiceNow can give customers a much more complete view of their entire system, both digital and physical.

That unlocks a few things. It strengthens ServiceNow's core database, improves its security offerings, and makes its end-to-end workflows more useful across industries. It also expands the company's addressable market into areas it hasn't fully tapped before.

There were some concerns around ServiceNow becoming more aggressive on acquisitions, but this deal seems more targeted than transformative.

data.title: Knightscope Inc reports results for the quarter ended December 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Knightscope Inc reported a quarterly adjusted loss of 92 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended December 31, lower than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of -78 cents. The mean expectation of three analysts for the quarter was for a loss of 75 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from -84 cents to -62 cents per share.

  • Revenue fell 9.8% to $2.54 million from a year ago; analysts expected $2.81 million.

  • Knightscope Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was a loss of 92 cents​.

  • The company reported a quarterly loss of $11.05 million.

  • Knightscope Inc shares had held steady so far this quarter and gained 12.4% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no earnings estimate revisions by analysts covering the company.

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 3 "strong buy" or "buy," no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the communications & networking peer group is also "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Knightscope Inc is $12.00, about 65.3% above its last closing price of $4.17

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data April 1 at 09:36 p.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: Australia's regulator flags governance, risk failures at ASX in final report
data.description: Australia's corporate regulator on Thursday flagged governance weaknesses and risk management failures at the country's market operator ASX in its final report following an independent inquiry.

data.title: Australian shares set to open higher, NZ up
data.description:
Australian shares were poised to open slightly higher on Thursday, buoyed by tentative hopes of de-escalation of the Iran war, with mining and gold stocks likely to gain support from strong underlying commodity prices.

The local share price index futures (YAPcm1) rose 0.2%, a 56.2-point premium to the underlying S&P/ASX 200 index close. The benchmark ended 2.2% higher on Wednesday.

New Zealand's benchmark S&P/NZX 50 index rose 0.2% in early trade.
